question: Which quarter appears to show the best overall performance based on the stacked bar chart of quarterly performance indicators for Store X?

options.A: Q2 shows the best performance because it has the highest total height across all stacked indicators.
options.B: The chart cannot determine overall performance accurately because it combines indicators with different units that cannot be meaningfully added together.
options.C: Q1 shows the best performance because it has the smallest bar overall.
options.D: Q4 shows the best performance because its electricity consumption segment is the lowest.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Store Visits, Electricity Consumption, etc.) into a single total. These metrics have different units and meanings (e.g., counts, temperatures, consumption), so adding them together creates a meaningless sum. The stacked height cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric comparisons are valid.
